title: Kalman/state-space latent tyre-degradation residual features
summary: Recent F1 work shows state-space tire models beat simpler AR baselines by estimating a latent degradation state with pit-stop resets.
rationale: Recent F1 work shows state-space tire models beat simpler AR baselines by estimating a latent degradation state with pit-stop resets. That is a direct match for this competition because the observed features already contain noisy lap-time and degradation proxies.
implementation_hint: Within each race-driver stint, fit a simple leakage-safe recursive filter using LapTime, LapTime_Delta, TyreLife, and Cumulative_Degradation to estimate latent wear level, latent wear slope, and one-step forecast error. Use only forward recursion inside each sequence; add filtered state, innovation, and state-reset features to the existing tabular model.
expected_effect: Captures hidden degradation pressure more cleanly than fixed compound life thresholds, especially when observed degradation is noisy or compound-dependent.
risk: Poorly initialized filters or using smoothed future-aware states would leak badly and overstate CV.
